title: pinus parviflora var. morrisonicola
intro: Pinus parviflora var. morrisonicola, also known as Taiwanese white pine, is a rare and endemic variant of the Japanese white pine (Pinus parviflora). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which make it useful for treating various health conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving cough and respiratory issues, and even showing potential in cancer prevention. In traditional Chinese medicine, Pinus parviflora var. morrisonicola is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
